creator: jb.1234abcd@gmail.com
text:
Supplement.

# rm /usr/ports/INDEX*
# find /tmp -name "*INDEX*"
#

# portsnap fetch update
...
Building new INDEX files... done.
# ls /usr/ports/INDEX*
/usr/ports/INDEX-7	/usr/ports/INDEX-8	/usr/ports/INDEX-9
# find /tmp -name "*INDEX*"
#

# portmaster -L --index | egrep '(ew|ort) version|total install'
/tmp/d-41418-index/INDEX-9.bz2                100% of 1620 kB  161 kBps 00m00s
	===>>> New version available: xlockmore-5.40_1
===>>> 536 total installed ports
	===>>> 1 has a new version available
# ls /usr/ports/INDEX*
/usr/ports/INDEX-7	/usr/ports/INDEX-9
/usr/ports/INDEX-8	/usr/ports/INDEX-9.bz2
# find /tmp -name "*INDEX*"
# portmaster -L --index-only | egrep '(ew|ort) version|total install'
	===>>> New version available: xlockmore-5.40_1
===>>> 536 total installed ports
	===>>> 1 has a new version available
#

Note:
I repeated the above up to 'portmaster -L --index ...', but instead I executed
these two entries in reverse order:

# portmaster -L --index-only | egrep '(ew|ort) version|total install'
/tmp/d-39507-index/INDEX-9.bz2                100% of 1620 kB  173 kBps
	===>>> New version available: xlockmore-5.40_1
===>>> 536 total installed ports
	===>>> 1 has a new version available
# ls /usr/ports/INDEX*
/usr/ports/INDEX-7	/usr/ports/INDEX-9
/usr/ports/INDEX-8	/usr/ports/INDEX-9.bz2
# find /tmp -name "*INDEX*"
# portmaster -L --index | egrep '(ew|ort) version|total install'
	===>>> New version available: xlockmore-5.40_1
===>>> 536 total installed ports
	===>>> 1 has a new version available
#

Note that both retrieve INDEX-9.bz2 into /tmp and rebuild INDEX-* in
/usr/ports/.

Conclusion:
The --index-only option seems to be redundant in both cases (there is
the same behavior to attempt to process INDEX files).

Now some actual package install testing.

# portmaster -PP /usr/ports/x11/xlockmore
...
===>>> Gathering dependency list for x11/xlockmore from ports
...

# portmaster --index -PP /usr/ports/x11/xlockmore
...
===>>> Gathering dependency list for x11/xlockmore from ports
...

NOTE: the last line "... from ports"

# portmaster --index-only -PP /usr/ports/x11/xlockmore
...
===>>> Gathering dependency list for x11/xlockmore from INDEX-9
...

NOTE: the last line "... from INDEX-9"

NOTE: also, both entries utilized /usr/ports data.

Conclusion:
but,  these lines contradict to what is in portmaster(8) - after all,
somebody said that "words have meaning":
--index
  use INDEX-[7-9] exclusively to check if a port is up to date
--index-only
  do not try to use /usr/ports. For updating ports when no
  /usr/ports directory is present the -PP|--packages-only option is
  required. See the ENVIRONMENT section below for additional
  requirements.

jb
